title: ⚔️ Chapter boss ⭐⭐⭐: a day in the city

questions.prompt: Complete: "___ Anis enjoy the London bus tour?" — "Yes, he did."
questions.explanation: The short answer "Yes, he did" tells us the question is a simple-past question: Did + subject + base verb → "Did Anis enjoy…?". "Was/Were" would need an -ing or an adjective, and "Does" is present.

questions.prompt: Complete: "When the rain started, Anis and Oliver ___ across Hyde Park."
questions.explanation: The walk was already in progress when the rain started, so it takes the past continuous: was/were + verb-ing. "Anis and Oliver" is plural → "were walking". "Was walking" is singular, "were walk" forgets the -ing, and "walked" loses the idea of an action in progress.

questions.prompt: Complete: "The weather in London was ___ than in Bizerte: grey and rainy."
questions.explanation: "Bad" is irregular: its comparative is "worse than" ✓. The common trap is "badder" or "more bad" — "bad" never follows the -er or the more… rule. "Worst" is the form for three or more things.

questions.prompt: Complete: "Anis ___ his breakfast when the taxi ___."
questions.explanation: The long background action (eating breakfast) takes the past continuous, and the short action that cuts into it (the taxi) takes the past simple: "was eating … arrived" ✓. The common trap is putting both verbs in the same tense — but here one action interrupts the other, so the tenses must differ.

questions.prompt: Complete: "The Underground was ___ than the bus, but it was much faster."
questions.explanation: "Expensive" is a long adjective (three syllables), so its comparative is "more expensive than" ✓. The common trap is "expensiver" — you never add -er to a long adjective. "Most expensive" is for three or more, and "more expensiver" doubles the comparison.

options.text: Anis was tired after the long flight.
The old streets were narrow and quiet.
Oliver was more taller than Anis.
The museum was huge and modern.

questions.explanation: This asks for the incorrect sentence. "More taller" doubles the comparison: with a short adjective you use ONLY -er, so it must be "Oliver was taller than Anis" ✓. The other three sentences use adjectives and the past of "be" correctly.
